task_description: Create a new spreadsheet, enter and merge centered text across the top row, and save it to the desktop as 123.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Microsoft Excel desktop shortcut: a green square icon with a white spreadsheet grid and a white 'X', labeled 'Excel 2016'. The icon is highlighted with a light selection rectangle, and the semitransparent red X is centered directly on this shortcut. In the full desktop view, this shortcut appears in the upper-left area among other desktop icons such as This PC, PowerPoint 2016, Network, Recycle Bin, Control Panel, Microsoft Edge, PotPlayer, Adobe Acrobat DC, and Word 2016 on a Windows 10 desktop.
caption.expectation: Microsoft Excel 2016 should launch and open its start screen or a blank workbook window.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the top-right corner of the Excel start window, with the semitransparent red X centered on the middle title-bar button marked with a small horizontal line, which is the Minimize control. Nearby are a question-mark help icon to the left and the Close X button to the right. In the full-screen view, Excel 2016 is open on its Chinese start screen over the Windows desktop, showing recent documents and template tiles.
caption.action: Click the Minimize button in the top-right title bar of the Excel window.
caption.expectation: The Excel window should minimize to the taskbar, revealing the desktop behind it.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Excel start screen's template gallery with the semitransparent red X centered on the thumbnail of a blank worksheet preview: a small grid with column letters A, B, and C and a selected cell outlined in green. This is a template tile object in the gallery, labeled below as the blank workbook option. In the full-screen view, Excel 2016 is open on its Chinese start page with recent documents on the left and multiple template tiles across the main area; the targeted tile is the first template in the top row.
caption.action: Click the blank workbook template tile.
caption.expectation: A new empty Excel workbook should open, replacing the start screen with the worksheet editing view.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the upper-left of the worksheet with cell A1 outlined in green as the active cell, and the text entry area displays "AA". The insertion is going into the selected grid cell and is also reflected in the formula bar area, indicating typed cell content rather than a search field. In the full-screen view, a blank Excel workbook is open on Sheet1 with the Home tab visible in the ribbon, and the rest of the worksheet is empty.
caption.action: Type "AAA" into the currently selected cell, replacing the current partial content "AA" with "AAA".
caption.expectation: The active cell should display "AAA" as its content, with the text visible in the cell and in the formula bar.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the worksheet grid near the top-left, with cell A1 containing the text "AAA" and outlined in green as the current selection. The semitransparent red X is centered on the blank worksheet cell immediately to the right, in column B row 1, indicating a click on a canvas object cell rather than a toolbar control. In the full-screen view, Excel is open to a blank workbook on Sheet1 with the Home tab visible; the only entered content is "AAA" in A1 and the rest of the sheet is empty.
caption.action: Click the blank cell B1 to the right of the selected cell containing "AAA".
caption.expectation: The selection should move from A1 to B1, highlighting B1 as the active cell and leaving "AAA" stored in A1.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the active worksheet cell A1 outlined in green with the text "AAA" inside it, and a semitransparent red drag path extending horizontally to the right across the first row, indicating a drag starting from A1 toward neighboring cells in row 1. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Excel is open to a blank workbook on Sheet1 with only cell A1 populated and the rest of the worksheet empty.
caption.action: Click-and-hold on the filled cell A1 containing "AAA", drag along the shown path to the right across the first row toward adjacent cells, then release.
caption.expectation: The drag should extend the selection across multiple cells in row 1 to the right of A1, preparing that horizontal range for filling or selection.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the ribbon control labeled with the Chinese text "合并后居中," which is the Merge & Center button in the Alignment group. Nearby are alignment icons and the "自动换行" wrap text control, confirming this is a top toolbar UI control rather than a worksheet cell. In the full-screen view, Excel is open to a blank workbook on Sheet1, and a horizontal range across row 1 from the left side of the sheet is selected, with "AAA" visible in the leftmost selected cell.
caption.action: Click the Merge & Center button labeled "合并后居中" on the ribbon.
caption.expectation: The selected cells in row 1 should be merged into one cell, and the content should appear centered within the merged cell.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the green ribbon tab labeled "文件" at the top-left of Excel, which is the File tab, a toolbar/menu control. Nearby are the "开始" tab and save/undo icons, confirming this is in the top ribbon area rather than the worksheet. In the full-screen view, Excel displays a blank workbook with a merged and centered cell across the first row containing "AAA", and the Home tab ribbon is currently visible.
caption.action: Click the "文件" tab on the top-left ribbon.
caption.expectation: Excel should switch from the worksheet view to the File backstage menu, opening file-related options such as save, open, and print.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the left-side green backstage menu item labeled "另存为," which is the Save As command; this is a UI control in the menu column. In the full-screen view, Excel is already in the File backstage area on the "信息" (Info) page, with workbook information shown in the main pane and the vertical green navigation menu on the left containing items such as 新建, 打开, 保存, 另存为, 另存为 Adobe PDF, and 打印.
caption.action: Click the "另存为" (Save As) item in the left backstage menu.
caption.expectation: Excel should open the Save As page, showing locations and options for saving the workbook under a chosen name or format.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the mint-green list item labeled "这台电脑" with a small monitor icon, indicating the This PC save location entry. This is a UI control in the Save As location list, not a blank area or canvas object. In the full-screen view, Excel is in the File backstage on the "另存为" page, with the left green navigation menu visible and the main pane showing save destinations including OneDrive, 这台电脑, 添加位置, and folders such as 文档 and 桌面.
caption.action: Click the "这台电脑" (This PC) location entry in the Save As page.
caption.expectation: Excel should select that save location and proceed to show local save choices or open the Save As file browser for saving to this PC.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the gray-highlighted folder entry labeled "桌面" with a yellow folder icon, indicating the Desktop save location item in the file destination list. In the full-screen view, Excel is in the green File backstage on the "另存为" page, with "这台电脑" selected in the left pane and local folders "文档" and "桌面" shown in the main area.
caption.expectation: Excel should select the Desktop location and open the save dialog or proceed to the Desktop folder for saving the workbook there.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The visible text entry area is the Save As dialog's filename field labeled "文件名(N):" near the bottom. The insertion caret is active in that box, and the text being entered is "123", appearing as the new filename content rather than appended to a longer existing name. In the full-screen view, Excel's save dialog is open to the Desktop folder under This PC, the file list area is empty, and below the filename field the save type dropdown shows "Excel 工作簿(*.xlsx)" with "保存" and "取消" buttons on the right.
caption.action: Type "123" into the "文件名(N):" filename field.
caption.expectation: The filename box should display "123", setting the workbook name to 123 while keeping the Excel workbook file type unchanged.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the "保存(S)" button, a standard dialog button in the bottom-right area of the Save As window, next to "取消" and near the "工具(L)" dropdown. In the full-screen view, a Windows "另存为" Save As dialog from Excel is open to the Desktop folder, the filename field contains "123", the file type is "Excel 工作簿(*.xlsx)", and the main file list is empty.
caption.expectation: The Save As dialog should close and Excel should save the workbook to the Desktop with the filename "123.xlsx".
